qa_pairs.question: How can I view the detailed list of my files that are scheduled for deletion from /scratch?
qa_pairs.answer: You can view the file listing all files scheduled for deletion using standard commands like `more`, `less`, or `cat`. For instance, `more /scratch/t/todelete/current/3110001___xxyz_______abc_________1.00T_____9560files` would display the contents of such a file (T:20).

qa_pairs.question: How do I check if other users in my group have files scheduled for purging from /scratch?
qa_pairs.answer: You can verify other users in your group with files scheduled for purging by using the `ls` command with `grep` on your group name. For example, `ls -1 /scratch/t/todelete/current |grep abc` would list all users in the 'abc' group with files to be purged (T:21).

qa_pairs.question: Do members of the same group have access to each other's contents on SciNet?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, members of the same group have access to each other's contents (T:21).
